net.datafaker.service

Class FakeValuesService

  • java.lang.Object
    • net.datafaker.service.FakeValuesService

  • public class FakeValuesService
extends java.lang.Object

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      FakeValuesService(java.util.Locale locale, RandomService randomService)
      Resolves YAML file using the most specific path first based on language and country code.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • FakeValuesService

        public FakeValuesService(java.util.Locale locale,
                         RandomService randomService)
        Resolves YAML file using the most specific path first based on language and country code. 'en_US' would resolve in the following order:
        1. /en-US.yml
        2. /en.yml
        The search is case-insensitive, so the following will all resolve correctly. Also, either a hyphen or an underscore can be used when constructing a Locale instance. This is legacy behavior and not condoned, but it will work.
        • EN_US
        • En-Us
        • eN_uS

    • Method Detail

      • addPath

        public void addPath(java.util.Locale locale,
                    java.nio.file.Path path)
        Allows adding paths to files with custom data. Data should be in YAML format.
        Parameters:
        locale - the locale for which a path is going to be added.
        path - path to a file with YAML structure
        Throws:
        java.lang.IllegalArgumentException - in case of invalid path

      • localeChain

        protected java.util.List<java.util.Locale> localeChain(java.util.Locale from)
        Convert the specified locale into a chain of locales used for message resolution. For example:

        Locale.FRANCE (fr_FR) to [ fr_FR, anotherTest, en ]

        Returns:
        a list of Locale instances

      • getLocalesChain

        public java.util.List<java.util.Locale> getLocalesChain()

      • fetch

        public java.lang.Object fetch(java.lang.String key)
        Fetch a random value from an array item specified by the key

      • fetchString

        public java.lang.String fetchString(java.lang.String key)
        Same as fetch(String) except this casts the result into a String.

      • safeFetch

        public java.lang.String safeFetch(java.lang.String key,
                                  java.lang.String defaultIfNull)
        Safely fetches a key.

        If the value is null, it will return an empty string.

        If it is a list, it will assume it is a list of strings and select a random value from it.

        If the retrieved value is an slash encoded regular expression such as /[a-b]/ then the regex will be converted to a regexify expression and returned (ex. #regexify '[a-b]')

        Otherwise it will just return the value as a string.

        Parameters:
        key - the key to fetch from the YML structure.
        defaultIfNull - the value to return if the fetched value is null
        Returns:
        see above

      • fetchObject

        public java.lang.Object fetchObject(java.lang.String key)
        Return the object selected by the key from yaml file.
        Parameters:
        key - key contains path to an object. Path segment is separated by dot. E.g. name.first_name

      • numerify

        public java.lang.String numerify(java.lang.String numberString)
        Returns a string with the '#' characters in the parameter replaced with random digits between 0-9 inclusive.

        For example, the string "ABC##EFG" could be replaced with a string like "ABC99EFG".

      • bothify

        public java.lang.String bothify(java.lang.String string)
        Applies both a numerify(String) and a letterify(String) over the incoming string.

      • bothify

        public java.lang.String bothify(java.lang.String string,
                                boolean isUpper)
        Applies both a numerify(String) and a letterify(String, boolean) over the incoming string.

      • regexify

        public java.lang.String regexify(java.lang.String regex)
        Generates a String that matches the given regular expression.

      • examplify

        public java.lang.String examplify(java.lang.String example)
        Generates a String by example. The output string will have the same pattern as the input string.

      • letterify

        public java.lang.String letterify(java.lang.String letterString)
        Returns a string with the '?' characters in the parameter replaced with random alphabetic characters.

        For example, the string "12??34" could be replaced with a string like "12AB34".

      • letterify

        public java.lang.String letterify(java.lang.String letterString,
                                  boolean isUpper)
        Returns a string with the '?' characters in the parameter replaced with random alphabetic characters.

        For example, the string "12??34" could be replaced with a string like "12AB34".

      • templatify

        public java.lang.String templatify(java.lang.String letterString,
                                   char char2replace,
                                   java.lang.String... options)
        Returns a string with the char2replace characters in the parameter replaced with random alphabetic characters from options

      • templatify

        public java.lang.String templatify(java.lang.String letterString,
                                   java.util.Map<java.lang.Character,java.lang.String[]> optionsMap)
        Returns a string with the optionsMap.getKeys() characters in the parameter replaced with random alphabetic characters from corresponding optionsMap.values()

      • resolve

        public java.lang.String resolve(java.lang.String key,
                                java.lang.Object current,
                                Faker root)
        Resolves a key to a method on an object or throws an exception.

        #{hello} with result in a method call to current.hello();

        #{Person.hello_someone} will result in a method call to person.helloSomeone();

      • resolve

        public java.lang.String resolve(java.lang.String key,
                                java.lang.Object current,
                                Faker root,
                                java.util.function.Supplier<java.lang.String> exceptionMessage)
        Resolves a key to a method on an object or throws an exception with specified message.

        #{hello} with result in a method call to current.hello();

        #{Person.hello_someone} will result in a method call to person.helloSomeone();

      • expression

        public java.lang.String expression(java.lang.String expression,
                                   Faker faker)
        Resolves an expression using the current faker.

      • fileExpression

        public java.lang.String fileExpression(java.nio.file.Path path,
                                       Faker faker)
        Resolves an expression in file using the current faker.

      • csv

        public java.lang.String csv(int limit,
                            java.lang.String... columnExpressions)
        Generates csv based on input column expressions and number of lines. This method uses default separator, quote and always prints header.

      • csv

        public java.lang.String csv(java.lang.String delimiter,
                            char quote,
                            boolean withHeader,
                            int limit,
                            java.lang.String... columnExpressions)
        Generates csv based on input.

      • json

        public Json json(java.lang.String... fieldExpressions)
        Generates json based on input.

      • jsona

        public Json jsona(java.lang.String... fieldExpressions)
        Generates json based on input.

      • resolveExpression

        protected java.lang.String resolveExpression(java.lang.String expression,
                                             java.lang.Object current,
                                             Faker root)
        processes a expression in the style #{X.y} using the current objects as the 'current' location within the yml file (or the Faker object hierarchy as it were).

        #{Address.streetName} would get resolved to Faker.address()'s Address.streetName() #{address.street} would get resolved to the YAML like locale: faker: address: street: Combinations are supported as well: "#{x} #{y}"

        Recursive templates are supported. if "#{x}" resolves to "#{Address.streetName}" then "#{x}" resolves to Faker.address()'s Address.streetName().

      • primitiveToWrapper

        public static java.lang.Class<?> primitiveToWrapper(java.lang.Class<?> cls)
